title: HBO Introduced A Permanent Change To A Song Of Ice And Fire Lore
summary: With the House of the Dragon premiere, HBO added an addendum to accepted A Song of Ice and Fire lore, and it looks like the change is here to stay.
imageAlt: HBO Introduced A Permanent Change To A Song Of Ice And Fire Lore
aiSummary: With the House of the Dragon premiere, HBO added an addendum to accepted A Song of Ice and Fire lore, and it looks like the change is here to stay.

whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de cultura.

fullArticle:
When HBO premiered House of the Dragon, viewers didn't realize that they would be witnessing a major, permanent change in lore to George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire universe. A Song of Ice and Fire has expanded in the years since Game of Thrones first premiered on HBO.

It's not just that there have been two major TV spinoffs, House of the Dragon and A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms; Martin has written several supplemental stories since 2011, including Fire & Blood, The World of Ice and Fire, and other lore-heavy texts. None of those mention what Viserys I (Paddy Considine) tells Rhaenyra (Milly Alcock) at the end of the HOTD premiere.

At the end of the episode, Viserys tells his daughter about a prophecy, passed down from Targaryen ruler to Targaryen ruler. It's called "the song o...
